* feat(AccountWidget): embedded app update flow with dialog, close, and open VS Code

- Show outlined 'Update Available' button when updates are disabled due to embedded app
- On click, confirm dialog explains Sessions will close and VS Code will open
- Opens VS Code via productService.urlProtocol with windowId=_blank (new empty window)
- Closes Sessions window after launching VS Code
- Uses secondary outlined button style (border, no fill) for hint state
- Inject IOpenerService, IDialogService, INativeHostService
- Remove simulation TODOs, use real updateService.state

* refactor(update): detect updates in embedded app via canInstall flag

- Add optional canInstall field to AvailableForDownload state
- Darwin: embedded app runs normal init + scheduled checks via HTTP
  (no Electron autoUpdater events), sets AvailableForDownload(update, false)
- Win32: embedded app skips platform setup, checks via HTTP,
  sets AvailableForDownload(update, false) when update found
- Sessions UI: check canInstall === false for hint button + dialog
- Remove DisablementReason.EmbeddedApp (no longer needed)
- Non-embedded --sessions mode uses standard update flow unchanged

* fix: update AccountWidget fixture with new constructor args

* fix: use IHostService instead of INativeHostService for browser layer compliance

commit.message:
fix: improve in-app block diagram and flowchart

Block diagram:
- Added layer labels (Hardware, Bridge, Dashboard)
- Added JSON fields preview strip (all 14 fields)
- Added REST endpoint badges on bridge server
- Added page badges on dashboard
- Added emojis and SRAM spec to Arduino block

Flowchart:
- Fixed purely-linear flow — now has YES/NO branching
- Decision diamonds fork into parallel paths
- Shows buzzer loop (keep beeping) vs silence alert
- Both paths merge back to JSON output
- Added loop indicator at bottom

feat: Universal Repo-Truth-Extractor Sync Pipeline (#177)

* fix(hygiene): update root hygiene policy to include missing allowed paths

* feat(extractor): v5 transition + absolute 100% coverage + safety mandates

- Systematically patched all prompt source scopes for absolute 100% repo coverage.
- Integrated modern architectural surfaces: Hooks, Agents, and Editor Configs (Vibe/Copilot).
- Registered 6 new integration steps and 7 artifacts in promptset/artifacts registries.
- Authored 6 new v5-style prompts for integration surface mapping.
- Added 'sync_prompt_scopes.py' tool and integrated '--sync' flag in runner.
- Codified 'NO LIVE RUNNER' safety mandate in GEMINI.md to prevent credit consumption.
- Fixed UnboundLocalError and registry referential integrity (PROMPTSET_INVALID).

* refactor(cli): extract 13 inline @cli.group() definitions into commands/ submodules

Split the monolithic cli.py (7402 lines) into focused command modules under
src/dopemux/commands/. Each group now lives in its own file with correct
relative imports (..module). cli.py reduced to ~4606 lines and uses
cli.add_command() for all groups.

New modules: autoresponder, capture_group, code, decisions, dev, extract,
extractor, memory, profile, trigger_group, update, upgrades, workflow_group.

Also: add Serena ~/.serena and host code volume mounts to compose.yml,
remove stale docker/mcp-servers-source/docker-compose.yml duplicate,
update test monkeypatches to target capture/trigger module namespaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): move autoresponder config subcommand into autoresponder_commands module

The config subcommand was left in cli.py after extraction, causing a
NameError for _get_autoresponder_config_manager which now lives in the
commands module. Move the command definition to the correct file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): move entry point block, update architecture docs, and stabilize workspace paths

* feat: universal repo-truth-extractor sync pipeline

Implement the core sync pipeline that enables the extractor to work on
any codebase, not just dopemux-mvp. The pipeline runs:

  fingerprint → auto-detect features → interactive discovery →
  resolve scopes → render templates → generate contracts → validate integrity

New modules in lib/promptgen/:
- feature_detector.py: 30 built-in rules detecting HTTP APIs, event systems,
  databases, CI, MCP, CLI, docs, testing, agents, plugins, monitoring, etc.
- phase_applicability.py: Phase inclusion/skip determination (16 phases)
  with step-level feature gates and force_include/force_skip overrides.
- scope_resolver.py: Per-step input scope resolution from features + profile.
- interactive_discovery.py: Developer-in-the-loop feature confirmation using
  Questionary with ADHD-friendly progressive disclosure.
- template_renderer.py: Jinja2-based prompt template rendering with
  validation against the 9-section contract.
- contract_generator.py: Dynamic promptset.yaml, artifacts.yaml, and
  model_map.yaml generation from pipeline artifacts.
- integrity_validator.py: Tier 0 referential integrity checks (hard gate)
  ensuring step_id alignment across all config files.
- sync_engine.py: Orchestrator chaining all stages, producing a complete
  generated promptset directory with SYNC_MANIFEST.json.

CLI rewrite (extractor_commands.py):
- `dopemux extractor init` — run the sync pipeline
- `dopemux extractor run` — execute extraction (with safety guard)
- `dopemux extractor status` — show generated promptset status
- `dopemux extractor validate` — run integrity validation

Tests: 57 tests covering all modules + full end-to-end pipeline.
Dogfood: Successfully synced against dopemux-mvp (27 features detected,
15 phases, 61 steps, 0 integrity errors).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: v5 --promptset-root flag, base templates, ADR-219, how-to docs

- Add --promptset-root CLI flag to run_extraction_v5.py that wires
  into REPO_TRUTH_EXTRACTOR_PROMPT_ROOT env var, enabling v5 to
  consume generated promptsets from the sync pipeline.

- Create 5 base prompt templates (A0, C0, D0, E0, G0) in
  base_prompts/ with Jinja2 template variables for repo-specific
  scope injection (repo.name, scopes, features conditionals).

- Write ADR-219 documenting the universal extractor sync pipeline
  architecture, three-tier prompt classification, and CLI surface.

- Write how-to guide for universal extractor usage covering init,
  validate, status commands, programmatic API, and custom feature rules.

Cross-repo validation: tested against dopemux-mvp (27 features,
15 phases), extractor self (10 phases), and ui-dashboard (2 features,
non-dopemux). All pass with 0 integrity errors.

feat(messaging): replace 5s poll loop with Redis pub/sub push delivery

- delivery.rs: after XADD to user Redis stream, PUBLISH to inbox:wakeup:{user_id}
  (fire-and-forget — non-critical, poll loop is the fallback)
- messaging-service: add spawn_inbox_wakeup() — subscribes to inbox:wakeup:{uid}
  channel via dedicated Redis pub/sub connection, forwards signals via mpsc
- MessageStream loop: adds wakeup arm to tokio::select! for immediate delivery
  when a message arrives; subscribes lazily if user_id not known at stream open
- Fallback poll interval: 5s → 60s (handles reconnects / missed pub/sub events)

Before: receipts and messages delivered with up to 5s latency; N streams = N
DB queries every 5 seconds. After: near-instant delivery via pub/sub push;
poll only fires as safety net every 60s.

commit.message:
Auto-add GDAL virtual filesystem prefixes for URLs, cloud storage, and archives (#144)

Adds a data-driven _gdal_path() function that automatically prepends
the appropriate /vsi*/ prefix:

- Cloud storage schemes (s3://, gs://, az://, oss://, swift://) are
  stripped and replaced with /vsis3/, /vsigs/, /vsiaz/, etc.
- Network URLs (http://, https://, ftp://) are prepended with /vsicurl/.
- Archive extensions (.zip, .gz, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .7z, .rar, .kmz,
  .ods, .xlsx, .lpk(x), .mpk(x), .ppkx) are wrapped with /vsizip/,
  /vsigzip/, /vsitar/, /vsi7z/, or /vsirar/. Extensions are detected
  at path boundaries to support subpaths (archive.zip/dir/file.shp).
- Prefixes chain when both apply, e.g.
  https://host/data.zip -> /vsizip//vsicurl/https://host/data.zip
- Existing /vsi* prefixes are left untouched.
- An @info message is logged when a path is rewritten.

The top-level read() also applies _gdal_path and forces ArchGDALDriver
for rewritten paths, since native drivers cannot handle /vsi* paths.

Adding new handlers requires only adding an entry to the lookup tables.

Fixes #143, fixes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(blazor): downgrade SignalR.Client 10.0.3 -> 9.0.3 (fixes circuit crash)

ROOT CAUSE: Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client 10.0.3 (.NET 10) was
referenced in a .NET 9 project. The .NET 10 MessagePackHubProtocolWorker
calls IInvocationBinder.GetTarget(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) which does not
exist in .NET 9's Blazor runtime, causing MissingMethodException wrapped
as InvalidDataException: Reading 'target' as String failed.

Also downgraded StackExchangeRedis 10.0.3 -> 9.0.3 for version consistency.

Verified with Playwright browser: circuit connects, buttons work, tabs
open/close, real-time data updates, navigation works. No console errors.

commit.message:
Fix duplicate client/operation names when operation IDs contain multiple underscores (#5348)

* Initial plan

* Fix duplicate class/method generation when operation IDs contain multiple underscores

Use the first underscore as the separator in MultipleClientsFromOperationIdOperationNameGenerator:
- GetClientName: everything before the first '_' = client name
- GetOperationName: everything after the first '_' = operation name

This guarantees uniqueness since OpenAPI requires globally unique operation IDs.
Previously, 'Orders_items_get' and 'Products_items_get' both produced
client='items', operation='get', causing duplicate generated code.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>
